title: Purely Smoked Syrian Chicken Kebabs
description: A sweet and juicy Syrian dish featuring sake and smoke, perfect for a hangover cure, cooked in a tandoor clay pot. This raw-friendly recipe is sure to delight.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together sake, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, paprika, salt, black pepper, and sumac.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers in the preheated tandoor clay pot and cook for 20 minutes, turning occasionally.
Brush the chicken with additional olive oil and sake during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Remove the skewers from the pot and sprinkle with chopped pine nuts.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with additional sumac and pine nuts if desired.
